title: Formidable Red Sox extend win streak to 11 games with doubleheader sweep of Rays
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Boston Red Sox extended their win streak to 11 games with a doubleheader sweep of the Tampa Bay Rays.
Starting pitcher Jake Bennett led the Game 1 shutout with six innings of one-hit baseball, securing his fifth win.
Boston achieved a .500 season record and holds the last AL wild-card spot, leading Seattle and Minnesota by half a game.

title: Chad Tracy offers updates on several injured Red Sox pitchers
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Ranger Suarez could start on July 21 after recovering from a left groin strain; Connelly Early shows progress but no return date.
Kutter Crawford is throwing from 100 feet; Tanner Houck is throwing off a mound twice weekly after Tommy John surgery.
Johan Oviedo to attempt a bullpen session; Roman Anthony, Garrett Crochet, and others remain sidelined with no return timeline.

title: 4 Red Sox who rose their stock following Friday’s doubleheader sweep vs. Rays
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Masataka Yoshida excelled in Red Sox's Game 1 win, going 3 for 4 with a home run and RBI.
Caleb Durbin impressed with a 5 for 7 performance, consistently reaching base to pressure Rays' pitching.
Jake Bennett pitched six scoreless innings, allowing one hit and walk, aiding bullpen conservation for Game 2.

title: Robert Kraft backs Mike Vrabel after controversial offseason: ‘No one is infallible’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots owner Robert Kraft supports coach Mike Vrabel amid offseason allegations involving reporter Dianna Russini.
Vrabel missed Day 3 of the NFL draft after addressing personal issues; Russini resigned from The Athletic.
Patriots begin training camp in Foxborough next week, following their Super Bowl appearance last season.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Anya Taylor-Joy stars in 'Lucky', a crime series available now on Apple TV, featuring a family drama over stolen money.
'Obsession', 2026 horror hit grossing over $400 million on a $750,000 budget, is streaming on Peacock.
'The Hunt for the Wilderpeople', featuring late Sam Neill, streams on Prime Video and Plex, showcasing a New Zealand adventure.

title: NH judge: Concord man violated Civil Rights Act in assault on transgender woman
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Travis Lufkin violated NH Civil Rights Act in a 2024 bias-motivated assault on a transgender woman in Concord.
Lufkin was ordered no contact with the victim for three years and fined $5,000, with $4,000 suspended.
He pleaded guilty to assault charges and received two consecutive 12-month sentences, both partly suspended.

title: Red Sox’ trade deadline plans ‘fluid,’ ESPN’s Jeff Passan says
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox have a 10-game win streak, improving their odds for the playoffs to 44.3%, per FanGraphs.
Boston's trade plans before the Aug. 3 deadline are fluid, according to ESPN's Jeff Passan.
The team is exploring adding a right-handed hitter, but All-Star closer Aroldis Chapman's trade is unlikely.

title: Popular Nantucket pizzeria Pi is for sale for $12.5 million
author: Madison Lucchesi
bullets: Pi Pizzeria in Nantucket is listed for sale at $12.5 million as owners Evan and Maria Marley retire.
The property for sale includes a 46-seat dining room, wood fire pizza oven, and staff housing with three bedrooms.
Evan Marley plans to assist in the transition to new ownership, with the restaurant continuing operations until sold.

title: France’s World Cup squad bids adieu to Boston
author: John Waller
bullets: France's World Cup team left Boston after over a month at the Four Seasons Hotel since early June.
Instead of the World Cup Final, France will play England in Miami for third place after losing to Spain.
Les Bleus rented the entire Four Seasons Hotel and practiced at Bentley University during their Boston stay.

title: Matt Damon says he’s working on a Karen Read movie
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Matt Damon's production company, Artists Equity, is in early stages of developing a movie about Karen Read's murder case.
Karen Read was acquitted of charges in the 2022 death of Boston Police Officer John O'Keefe; the case remains controversial.
Other adaptations include a Lifetime movie, a Prime Video series starring Elizabeth Banks, and a Netflix documentary series.
